summary: AST-based project skeleton generator that extracts function/class signatures from source files across multiple programming languages. Produces a project API surface file and call-graph neighborhoods for map agents.

findings.description: Assumes all paths in list are children of root; if any path is not a descendant, relative_to() will raise ValueError
If path.read_text() fails due to permissions or encoding issues (even with errors='replace'), the SyntaxError catch won't handle OSError
Condition for detecting top-level functions is flawed; it checks if any node in the entire tree is a FunctionDef (always true for the file itself), causing only class methods to be captured
Assumes ast.unparse() is available; older Python versions (<3.9) will raise AttributeError
ImportFrom may have module=None for 'from . import x'; appending '.alias.name' creates leading dot which may break downstream matching
File opened but not explicitly closed; relies on garbage collection. Large projects may have many files open simultaneously.
Assumes imported module names map cleanly to file paths via replace('/', '.') removal of .py suffix; doesn't handle __init__.py packages correctly
No error handling for output_path.write_text(); if output directory doesn't exist or lacks permissions, entire operation fails
Assumes SKIP_DIRS contains all common build/artifact directories; may miss project-specific directories like 'target', 'out', 'bin'
findings.evidence:
if any(part in SKIP_DIRS for part in p.parts):
    continue

interfaces_exported: build_neighborhood(target: Path, repo_root: Path, source_files: list[Path]) -> str
explicit_requirements: Produces two artifacts: skeleton.txt and neighborhood
Python files are parsed with ast module

implicit_assumptions.assumption: All files are UTF-8 encoded or can be read with errors='replace' without losing critical structure
implicit_assumptions.risk: Binary files or other encodings may cause regex patterns to match garbage or fail entirely

implicit_assumptions.assumption: Project structure is flat enough that relative_to() won't fail (all source files are under repo_root)
implicit_assumptions.risk: If collect_source_files returns paths outside repo_root (symlinks), _build_tree_lines crashes

implicit_assumptions.assumption: Import statements in Python files are correctly formatted and parseable by ast
implicit_assumptions.risk: Dynamic imports or conditional imports may be missed, leading to incomplete neighborhood

implicit_assumptions.assumption: Module names map 1:1 to file paths with simple dot/ slash conversion
implicit_assumptions.risk: Namespace packages, __init__.py, or custom sys.path modifications cause import matching failures

implicit_assumptions.assumption: Regex patterns are sufficient for extracting signatures from all supported languages
implicit_assumptions.risk: Multi-line signatures, decorators, or language-specific quirks cause missed signatures

cross_file_flags: garlicpress/map_agent.py consumes build_skeleton and build_neighborhood; verify assumptions about format match expectations
garlicpress/cli.py calls write_skeleton; ensure output directory exists before calling
garlicpress/queue.py may be used by other modules that assume consistent file IDs; ensure skeleton doesn't affect file identification
